source: SimCLR - A Simple Framework for Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations

concepts.finding: SimCLR: simple contrastive learning framework. Key components: (1) composition of data augmentations critical, (2) learnable nonlinear projection head improves representation quality, (3) contrastive loss (NT-Xent) with large batch size and many negatives, (4) normalized embeddings + temperature scaling. Outperforms supervised methods on ImageNet with linear probe.
concepts.implication: Contrastive keyword embedding: two augmented views of same keyword (e.g., co-occurrence neighborhood dropout, synonym substitution) should have similar embeddings. Projection head (MLP) followed by contrastive loss. Large batch size important for many negatives.
concepts.open_question: What keyword data augmentations produce effective positive pairs? Can SimCLR-style contrastive learning produce keyword embeddings without any labeled intents?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: SimCLR-trained keyword embeddings (with neighborhood sampling and synonym substitution as augmentations) outperform word2vec embeddings for downstream intent clustering tasks.
